Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additively manufactured heterogeneous precipitation-strengthened high-entropy alloys with high strength and ductility

Composition_Info.Note: Overall composition of AP-HEA measured by SEM-EDS, close to designed composition Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3

Process_Text.original: Elemental powders of Co, Ni, Cr, Fe, Al, and Ti were mechanically mixed via three-dimensional mixer. DED processing using fiber laser (wavelength 1080 nm, spot diameter 1.2 mm), laser power 750 W, scanning speed 480 mm/min, layer thickness 0.3 mm, powder feeding rate 14.45 g/min, hatch distance 0.8 mm, argon gas protection.
Process_Text.simplified: DED of CoNiCrFeAlTi HEA using elemental powder blend, 750 W laser power, 480 mm/min scanning speed

Process_Info.Equipment: DED system with fiber laser (wavelength 1080 nm, spot diameter 1.2 mm) and coaxial powder feeding system

Microstructure_Text.original: Typical cellular structure (~5 μm diameter) with FCC crystal structure. Ti-rich zone observed in the wall based on EDS mapping. High-density dislocations generated in both interior (A) and wall (B) regions due to repeated compression-tension cycles during laser scanning. SAED patterns along [110] zone axis confirm FCC structure in both regions.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine cellular structure (~5 μm) with FCC matrix, Ti segregation in walls, high-density dislocations

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Flat dog-bone-shaped, gauge length 12.5 mm, cross-section 3.2×1.5 mm^2
Properties_Info.Note: Tensile direction vertical to building direction

Composition_Info.Note: Overall composition close to designed composition Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3; detailed phase compositions provided in Table 1 for L12 precipitates and FCC matrix in interior and wall regions

Process_Text.original: Elemental powders of Co, Ni, Cr, Fe, Al, and Ti were mechanically mixed via three-dimensional mixer. DED processing using fiber laser (wavelength 1080 nm, spot diameter 1.2 mm), laser power 750 W, scanning speed 480 mm/min, layer thickness 0.3 mm, powder feeding rate 14.45 g/min, hatch distance 0.8 mm, argon gas protection. Subsequent isothermal aging at 800°C for 4 h, then cooled to room temperature in air.
Process_Text.simplified: DED of CoNiCrFeAlTi HEA using elemental powder blend, 750 W laser power, 480 mm/min scanning speed, followed by aging at 800°C for 4 h (air cooled)

Process_Info.Equipment: DED system with fiber laser (wavelength 1080 nm, spot diameter 1.2 mm) and coaxial powder feeding system

Microstructure_Text.original: Heterogeneous cellular structure consisting of single-phase (FCC) interior and dual-phase (FCC + L12) wall. High-density nanoscale L12 precipitates observed in the wall. Average L12 precipitate size 31.0 ± 5.1 nm from DF-TEM imaging. FCC structure confirmed in interior by SAED along [110] zone axis. L12 precipitates in wall confirmed by superlattice reflections in DF-TEM. Ti, Al, Ni partition into L12 precipitates; Co, Cr, Fe partition into FCC matrix. Solvus temperature of L12 in interior ~797°C, in wall ~948°C.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Cellular structure with FCC interior and FCC+L12 dual-phase wall; ~31 nm L12 precipitates in wall (vol% ~20.8)
